page.title: Cleanup
page.subtitle: See how much space you're using and keep R2 Storage and the Database free of old dumps and leftovers.
section.storage.title: Storage
section.storage.description: How much space your dumps take in R2, grouped by connection.
section.retention.title: Retention policy
section.retention.description: Configure per connection and dump type how many days to retain. With space-to-free preview and manual execution.
section.health.title: System health
section.health.description: Background tasks to keep the Database and R2 Storage clean. You'll rarely need them.
table.caption: R2 storage usage per connection
detail.title: Detail by connection
empty.noDumps: You don't have any dumps yet. When a backup runs, you'll see them here.
manual.title: Manual dumps
manual.description: They don't have a cronjob, so they're cleaned with this policy once a day. At least 1 is always kept.
manual.activeNotice: Automatic cleanup ENABLED — runs every day at 03:00.
manual.enable: Enable automatic cleanup of manual dumps
manual.keepLastHint: Number of dumps to keep. 0 = no limit.
manual.maxAgeHint: Dumps older than this value will be deleted.
manual.maxSizeHint: Dumps will be deleted until the total drops below this cap.
form.hint.keepLast: All dumps except the most recent ones will be deleted.
form.hint.maxAge: Dumps older than the indicated value will be deleted.
form.preview.choose: Choose connection, type and criterion to see what would be deleted.
form.hint.noConnection: Select a connection and dump type first
form.hint.nothingToDelete: No dumps to delete with these criteria
confirm.delete.title: Delete dumps
confirm.delete.description: You are about to delete {{count}} dump(s) ({{totalSizeMb}} MB) from R2 and the database. This action is irreversible.
retention.title: Retention policy
retention.description: Automatically delete dumps by age according to their type. This policy only applies to the selected connection.
retention.policyApplied: Policy saved — applied in the next sweep.
retention.impact.title: Cleanup impact
retention.impact.description: What the current policy would delete if run now. Nothing is deleted — it's just a preview.
retention.impact.empty: No dumps exceed the configured age. Nothing to delete.
retention.confirm.title: Run cleanup now
retention.confirm.description: Dumps exceeding the configured age will be permanently deleted from R2 and the database. The history record is preserved. Continue?
toast.policySavedDesc: Retention will be applied in the next daily sweep.
toast.policyValidation: The value for "{{category}}" must be an integer ≥ 1.
hygiene.title: Old failed backups
hygiene.description: Each failed attempt leaves a record in the database. Delete the oldest ones so they don't accumulate. Your dumps are not touched.
hygiene.statusInvalid: Enter a valid number of days.
hygiene.statusNone: No failed records with that age.
hygiene.confirm.title: Delete failed records
hygiene.confirm.description: You are about to delete {{count}} failed backup record(s) older than {{days}} day(s). This action is irreversible.
reconcile.title: Sync Storage and Database
reconcile.description: Detects leftovers when R2 and the database get out of sync. What can be restored is never touched.
reconcile.allSynced: All synced — no leftovers to clean.
reconcile.confirm.title: Clean leftovers
reconcile.confirm.description: You are about to clean {{count}} leftover(s) from the database and R2: orphaned records, loose metadata, and incomplete dumps. Restorable dumps are not touched. This action is irreversible.
toast.reconcileSuccessWithErrors: {{db}} records · {{manifests}} metadata · {{dumps}} dumps cleaned · {{count}} with errors
